2605.23069.title: DFKI-MLT at SemEval-2026 TASK 7: Steering Multilingual Models Towards Cultural Knowledge
2605.23069.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used across diverse linguistic and cultural contexts, yet their cultural knowledge remains uneven across regions and languages. We present the DFKI-MLT system for SemEval-2026 Task 7 on cultural awareness, where we apply activation steering to multilingual LLMs using language vectors extracted from parallel FLORES data. Our method performs inference-time adaptation by adding language-specific steering vectors to the residual stream at a selected transformer layer, without any parameter updates. We participated in both the short-answer (SAQ) and multiple-choice (MCQ) tracks; however, only our MCQ submission received an official score. In the official MCQ track, we achieved 86.96% accuracy, ranking 7th out of 17 teams. To better understand system behavior, we conduct post-hoc analyses on the shared-task MCQ and SAQ settings. These analyses show that activation steering yields modest and heterogeneous improvements on cultural reasoning: gains are strongly layer-sensitive, vary substantially across language-region pairs, with some configurations even degrading performance, and interact with prompt formulation, comparing generic and culturally conditioned prompts. Our findings suggest that prompt design and activation steering should be jointly optimized for culturally aware multilingual inference.
2605.23069.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在多樣的語言和文化背景中越來越被廣泛使用，但它們的文化知識在不同地區和語言之間仍然不均衡。我們提出了DFKI-MLT系統，用於SemEval-2026第7任務的文化意識，在此我們利用從平行FLORES數據中提取的語言向量對多語言LLMs進行激活引導。我們的方法通過在選定的Transformer層中將語言特定的引導向量添加到殘差流中來執行推理時的適應，而無需任何參數更新。我們參加了短答案（SAQ）和多選題（MCQ）兩個賽道；然而，只有我們的MCQ提交獲得了官方分數。在官方MCQ賽道中，我們達到了86.96%的準確率，排名17支隊伍中的第7位。為了更好地理解系統行為，我們對共享任務的MCQ和SAQ設置進行了事後分析。這些分析顯示，激活引導在文化推理上產生了適度且異質的改善：增益對層次非常敏感，在語言-地區對之間變化顯著，某些配置甚至會降低性能，並且與提示的制定互動，對比通用和文化條件提示。我們的發現表明，提示設計和激活引導應該共同優化，以實現文化意識的多語言推理。
2605.23069.author: Yusser Al Ghussin et.al.
2605.23069.authors: Yusser Al Ghussin, Daniil Gurgurov, Yasser Hamidullah, Josef van Genabith, Cristina España-Bonet, Simon Ostermann
